exemplar.title:
How does De Beers use the interplay of visuals and texts to associate
diamonds to marriage?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does De Beers use the interplay of visuals and texts to associate
diamonds to marriage?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Introduction effectively contextualizes all three adverts with dates and thematic focus, demonstrating secure subject knowledge and a clear link to the line of inquiry.
Strong integration of visual details (colour contrast, exact weights) deepens interpretation, especially of the 1955 advert.
References and textual support are generally relevant and effectively tied to analytical points.
weaknesses: Occasional slips into pure description hinder the consistently persuasive interpretation required for the highest band.
Lack of full citations and captions under images reduces the precision of textual support.
Reference list is incompleteâadditional source details and attributions are needed for comprehensive coverage.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The response shows good, sustained knowledge and understanding of all three advertisements, with frequent and effective textual references. Interpretations of visual and lexical choices are convincing overall, matching the descriptor for band 4. While the work rarely delves into the consistently persuasive depth of band 5, the quality of contextual knowledge and textual support clearly outperforms lower bands.

strengths: Insightful analysis of enjambment and geological time in the 2018 advert strengthens the argument about eternal love and brand messaging.
Personification of nature (âmountains, sun, budsâ) is well observed and enriches the symbolic link between the diamond and transient aspects of love.
The juxtapositions in the 2018 ad are analyzed with strong insight into parallel structure and narrative impact.
weaknesses: Some claims (e.g., plosives mimicking shininess) remain speculative without a clear linguistic framework.
Rhetorical effect of alliteration in the 4Cs slogan is identified but not fully explored for audience impact.
Final reflective question invites critique but lacks a deeper evaluation of consumerism versus symbolism.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay offers appropriate and at times genuinely insightful analysis of textual features and authorial choices, aligning with the band 4 descriptor. Analysis moves well beyond mere description, though it occasionally stops short of fully substantiated evaluation, preventing it from reaching the consistently convincing insight of band 5.

strengths: Clear line of inquiry is announced and developed through three coherent thematic sections (value, nature metaphors, bonding function).
Logical paragraphing and use of signposting words (âLikewise,â âOn the other handâ) maintain cohesion across different adverts.
Supporting examples (quotations and visual details) are mostly well integrated into sentences and analytical discussion.
weaknesses: Transitions between some sections are abrupt, and a few paragraphs cut off mid-analysis, undermining full cohesion.
Thesis statement and roadmap could be more concise and explicit in outlining thematic sections.
Inconsistent inâtext labeling of visuals (Image 1, Image 2, etc.) reduces clarity of reference.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay is well organized and mostly cohesive, with an adequately developed line of inquiry throughout, meeting band 4. Examples are generally integrated into the argument, though occasional structural lapses and incomplete transitions prevent the seamless organization required for band 5.

strengths: Analytical register and formal academic style are consistently appropriate to the task, avoiding personal anecdote and maintaining focus.
Vocabulary is broadly varied and sentence structures are varied, contributing to clear communication of ideas.
Formatting and terminological choices (e.g., technical terms like âlower-angle shot,â âmodalityâ) support the analytical tone.
weaknesses: Persistent minor grammatical errors (subject-verb agreement, article omission, incorrect plurals) detract from precision.
Occasional run-on sentences and verbosity obscure clarity in places.
Citation formatting (URLs, reference details) is inconsistent and does not fully meet IB conventions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Language is clear with good accuracy in grammar and vocabulary overall, and the register is consistently appropriate, satisfying the band 4 descriptor. Although there are persistent minor errors and formatting issues, they do not significantly impede comprehension or the formal tone of the essay.

strandSelections.reasoning: Quotations and visual details are **mostly integrated smoothly** into sentences: e.g., the student embeds slogans within analytical clauses and blends image description with commentary.  Occasional awkward phrasing or lengthy quotations that stand alone prevent flawless integration, yet the standard is still âmostly well integratedâ, meriting band 4.

The student demonstrates **secure contextual knowledge** of all three advertisements: dates, target audiences, socio-economic aspirations and the long-running âA Diamond is Foreverâ campaign are explicitly stated and linked to the line of inquiry.  Discussion moves beyond surface description (e.g. locating the 4Cs slogan historically, explaining De Beersâ monopoly and salary-rule) and interprets why particular visual or lexical choices would persuade middle-class couples.  Although the commentary occasionally slips into description, the through-line of how diamonds become synonymous with marriage is maintained and interpretations are generally convincing, meeting the descriptor of sustained, good understanding rather than the consistently persuasive depth required for band 5.

A clear line of inquiry is announced, then developed through three thematic sections (value, nature metaphors, bonding function) that cover all images.  Paragraphing is logical, and signposting words (âLikewiseâ, âOn the other handâ) maintain cohesion.  Though some transitions are abrupt, the overall structure is **well organised and mostly cohesive** with adequate development of each thematic thread, fulfilling band 4 requirements.

Specific visual and textual details from each advert are **frequently quoted or paraphrased** (âchoose by colour, cutting, clarityâ¦â, âtwo monthsâ salaryâ, âTRUE LOVE WILL FIND A WAYâ) and embedded inside the analysis.  The student also comments on layout, colour contrast and camera angle.  These references are clearly tied to analytical points and therefore support ideas effectively.  Precision is, however, uneven: some descriptions of imagery (âlower-angle shotâ, âmonostitchâ) lack exact citation or illustrative figures, preventing the consistently exemplary selection expected in band 5.

The commentary maintains an appropriate analytical register, avoiding personal anecdote and using formal terminology (âenjambmentâ, âlower-angle shotâ, âhigh modalityâ).  Style is consistent and suited to an academic essay, though an occasional rhetorical question in the conclusion slightly relaxes formality.  Overall the work demonstrates **consistently appropriate register and style**, fitting band 4.

The language is **clear and readable**, with academic vocabulary and varied sentence structures.  However, there are persistent minor errors (subject-verb disagreement, missing articles, muddled idiom) and occasional verbosity that cloud precision.  These lapses keep accuracy at an âadequateâ rather than âgoodâ level, corresponding to band 3.

The essay moves beyond mere description by examining rhetorical devices (alliteration, plosives, enjambment), modality, financial lexis, juxtaposition of time scales, and camera angles.  These observations are **generally appropriate and relevant** to the purpose of the campaign.  Nonetheless, commentary often stops at identification with limited exploration of *how* such choices construct meaning, and some claims (âplosives mimic shininessâ) feel speculative.  Insight appears sporadically rather than consistently, aligning with the âgenerally appropriateâ wording of band 3.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively contextualizes the three adverts with dates and thematic focus, demonstrating good subject knowledge and a clear link to the line of inquiry.
The stated line of inquiry could be more concise and explicitly reference the analytical approach (value, metaphor, bonding) to guide the reader through the essayâs structure.
This opening paragraph contains a run-on sentence that obscures clarity. Consider splitting after 'middle class' and trimming redundant phrases for greater precision.
The thesis statement could more explicitly outline the three thematic sections you will cover, improving the essayâs roadmap and coherence.
The visuals on this page lack explicit in-text labels (e.g., Image 1, Image 2). Consistent labeling will help readers connect analysis to specific adverts.
Consider adding brief captions under each image to note the year, visual features, and purpose, reinforcing the link between image and analysis.
The caption 'Image 3, 2018' is minimal; expand it to include context (e.g., source, campaign tagline) to strengthen textual support.
You note the alliteration of 'c' in the 4Cs slogan, but the analysis misses how this device influences audience perception; explain its rhetorical effect.
The sentence ending with 'In stating' abruptly cuts off. Ensure each paragraph completes its argument or link to the next for textual cohesion.
Subject-verb agreement error: 'The value of diamonds ... are important'. It should read 'The value ... is important' to maintain grammatical accuracy.
Strong integration of visual details (color contrast, exact weights) shows good textual support and deepens your interpretation of the 1955 advert.
The claim that the plosives in 'big' and 'beautiful' mimic shininess is speculative; provide a linguistic or semiotic framework to justify this link.
Insightful analysis of enjambment and geological time in the 2018 ad strengthens the argument about eternal love and brand messaging.
The paragraph beginning 'On the other hand' cuts off mid-analysis. Complete the description of the solar eclipse imagery and its contrast with the diamond.
The plural 'jargons' is incorrectâ'jargon' is an uncountable noun. Review terminology usage for accurate language.
The personification of nature ('mountains, sun, buds') is well observed and enriches the symbolic link between the diamond and transient aspects of love.
The pronoun analysis in the 1983 section effectively connects linguistic choice to audience empathy, illustrating good integration of examples.
Transitions between the 1983 and 2018 analyses feel abrupt; add signposting phrases or linking sentences to maintain cohesion.
The series of juxtapositions in the 2018 ad is well analyzed, demonstrating strong insight into parallel structure and its narrative weight.
The concluding rhetorical question undercuts the analytical tone; consider replacing it with a concise evaluative summary.
The Business Insider statistic is strong support, but the reference lacks author, date, and proper formattingârevise to a consistent citation style.
The sentence ending with 'had' is incomplete, leaving the reader without closure. Complete the thought or restructure the sentence.
The comma after 'Though' in the conclusion is misplaced; consider removing or repositioning it for smooth sentence flow.
The reflective question invites critique but would benefit from a brief evaluation of consumerism vs. symbolism to deepen your final commentary.
The reference list includes only one source; add full details for all visual and secondary sources to ensure comprehensive attribution.
The YouTube reference is missing proper title formatting and access date; follow IB MLA/APA conventions for digital sources.
The URL is presented without a hanging indent or italics; adjust formatting to differentiate it from running text and meet style guidelines.
